title: Misleading warnings about use of 'libpod.conf'?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

If running podman with '`--log-level debug`' then amongst the output appears:

```
time="2020-07-13T14:16:37Z" level=debug msg="Found deprecated file /etc/containers/libpod.conf, please remove. Use /etc/containers/containers.conf to override defaults.\n"
```

... but if this advice is followed then none of the settings in `containers.conf` are applied - it very much looks as if podman is only examining `libpod.conf`, despite this notice.

Alternatively, is `containers.conf` of a different format to `libpod.conf` and, if so, could a link to further information or a migration guide be added to the debug message?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.2
Git Commit:   201c9505b88f451ca877d29a73ed0f1836bb96c7
Built:        Wed Jul  8 17:39:51 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
**Update:**

If I make `/etc/containers/libpod.conf` a symlink to `/etc/containers/containers.conf` then the settings from this file _are_ applied - suggesting that the debug line requesting the use of `containers.conf` has been added before `containers.conf` is being used in place of `libpod.conf`?

Containers.conf should override libpod.conf, if they do not then this is a bug.
The order should be 

DefaultConfig (Hard coded into Podman)
libpod.conf
/usr/share/containers/containers.conf
/etc/containers/containers.conf
$HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf
If you put a field in containers.conf, do you see the value from libpod.conf?
I thought we agreed the opposite, at least for the initial few releases that included containers.conf. We're at the point where I'd be comfortable switching to the opposite, though.

Well you should not have a containers.conf with any content, if you create one, and we accidently created a libpod.conf as a side effect, then the containers.conf should override the conflicting fields.

containers.conf that gets installed in /usr/share/containers/containers.conf is commented out.  I guess a distribution could screw this up, but for the most part I think this is the proper sequence.

> Well you should not have a containers.conf with any content, if you create one, and we accidently created a libpod.conf as a side effect, then the containers.conf should override the conflicting fields.
> 
> containers.conf that gets installed in /usr/share/containers/containers.conf is commented out. I guess a distribution could screw this up, but for the most part I think this is the proper sequence.

The message says to put settings/overrides in `/etc/containers/containers.conf` and that `/etc/containers/libpod.conf` should be migrated/removed... is this correct?

Yes.

Right now, `libpod.conf` takes precedence over `containers.conf`. `libpod.conf` is considered deprecated and this behavior will be switched (potentially soon) to the opposite, with libpod.conf settings being overridden by containers.conf if both are present. I don't think we're going to remove it entirely any time soon, but that too will probably happen eventually.
Yes remove libpod.conf and move any special settings to containers.conf, if they were something you set, if not, then removing libpod.conf should be enough.

If I remove `libpod.conf` and leave `containers.conf`, then at least some of the settings aren't applied:

```
dellr330 ~ # cd /etc/containers/
dellr330 containers # ls -l *.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5637 Jul 15 11:31 containers.conf
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root   15 Jul  9 12:22 libpod.conf -> containers.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  225 Jun  7 00:47 registries.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  788 May 25 21:25 storage.conf
dellr330 containers # podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                 COMMAND               CREATED       STATUS           PORTS                   NAMES
38a0acc8fbb9  localhost/dell-ism:351                                                      7 days ago    Up 7 days ago                            dell-ism-351
483e72e903b6  localhost/service.net-im.bitlbee:3.6-r1               -D -P /var/run/bi...  18 hours ago  Up 18 hours ago  0.0.0.0:6667->6667/tcp  openrc-bitlbee-3.6-r1
74e70f86157c  localhost/service.net-mail.imapproxy:1.2.8_p14843-r1  -f /etc/imapproxy...  31 hours ago  Up 31 hours ago                          openrc-imapproxy-1.2.8_p14843-r1
a76ba07c002f  localhost/service.net-misc.openntpd:6.2_p3-r1         -d -f /etc/opennt...  18 hours ago  Up 18 hours ago  0.0.0.0:123->123/udp    openrc-openntpd-6.2_p3-r1
cbad730605e7  localhost/service.net-misc.dhcp:4.4.2-r2              -cf /etc/dhcp/dhc...  29 hours ago  Up 29 hours ago                          openrc-dhcp-4.4.2-r2
e8af63ed9fd2  localhost/service.net-dns.bind:9.14.12                -u named -f           18 hours ago  Up 18 hours ago                          openrc-bind-9.14.12
eccca3b4dffa  localhost/service.sys-apps.irqbalance:1.6.0           -d -f -s /var/run...  18 hours ago  Up 18 hours ago                          openrc-irqbalance-1.6.0
f10c04ad9233  localhost/service.dev-lang.php:7.4.6                  --fpm-config /etc...  31 hours ago  Up 31 hours ago                          openrc-php-7.4.6
dellr330 containers # mv libpod.conf libpod.conf.disabled
dellr330 containers # podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
dellr330 containers # 
```
Are you saying  you have a setting in containers.conf that is being ignored by podman?  If so, what setting?

> Are you saying you have a setting in containers.conf that is being ignored by podman? If so, what setting?

Given that `podman` loses state when `libpod.conf` is removed with `containers.conf` in place, that seems to be a fair conclusion, no?

(Noting that `libpod.conf` is simply a symlink to `containers.conf`)

My `libpod.conf`/`containers.conf` reads:
```
image_default_transport = "docker://"
conmon_path = ["/usr/bin"]
conmon_env_vars = ["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
lock_type = "shm"
hooks_dir=["/etc/containers/hooks"]
static_dir = "/space/podman/static"
tmp_dir = "/var/run/libpod"
max_log_size = -1
no_pivot_root = false
cni_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"
cni_plugin_dir = ["/opt/cni/bin"]
infra_image = "gcr.io/google_containers/pause-amd64:3.2"
infra_command = "/usr/bin/pause"
namespace = "podman"
num_locks = 2048
volume_path = "/space/podman/volumes"
network_cmd_path = "/usr/bin/slirp4netns"
events_logger = "file"
cni_default_network = "podman"
enable_port_reservation = true
runtime = "runc"
runtime_supports_json = ["runc", "crun"]
runtime_supports_nocgroups = ["crun"]
[runtimes]
runc = ["/usr/bin"]
crun = ["/usr/bin"]
kata-runtime = []
kata-qemu = []
kata-fc = []
```

... so `static_dir` or `volume_path`?  Is the former path used when tracking active containers?
The formatting of the two files is somewhat different, so it's likely that some/all of the configuration directives of libpod.conf are being discarded after the rename.

> The formatting of the two files is somewhat different, so it's likely that some/all of the configuration directives of libpod.conf are being discarded after the rename.

Ahh - in that case some clarification of the debug might be helpful to confirm that manual migration is required, as I read it to be a simple rename.

(Also, if https://github.com/containers/common/blob/master/pkg/config/containers.conf is the correct resource, then the only actual difference seems to be that `containers.conf`subdivides settings into tables, whereas with `libpod.conf` all settings are at the top-level.  So they do seem similar enough to be easily mistaken, at a glance...)
Yes containers.conf coveres more then libpod.conf, so need to be careful, we want to make libpod.conf disappear.  Fixing up man pages should be next step.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
